title: ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. kilimandscharicum
intro: Ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. kilimandscharicum, commonly known as the African Adder's Tongue, is a subspecies of the adder's tongue fern, a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Ophioglossaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. kilimandscharicum include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally to reap its health benefits.
